tile.title: Depth-Weighted Self-Supervision: Physics-Driven Camera Selection
tile.content: SonarVision's self-supervision eliminates manual labeling by exploiting the physical arrangement of cameras at known depths. When sonar detects a target at depth D, the camera nearest to D automatically becomes the ground truth source. The depth-weighted loss: L = Σ_c exp(-|d_cam_c - d_sonar|² / 2σ²) × L1(predicted, camera_c) + λ × MSE(predicted_depth, sonar_depth). σ=3.0m means cameras within ±3m of the detection get >60% weight, cameras >9m away get <1%. This is a form of multi-view consistency loss where view selection is physics-driven rather than learned. Additional losses: TemporalConsistencyLoss (L1 between consecutive predicted frames), TurbidityAwareLoss (weight reduction in high-turbidity conditions). Data augmentation: SonarNoiseAugmentation (Gaussian + speckle + ping dropout), DepthJitter (±0.5m camera position uncertainty), TurbidityAugmentation (random haze), ColorShiftAugmentation (red attenuation + blue boost variation). The result: a system that learns from real sonar-camera pairs without any human annotation.
